message:
feat(scheduler): implement Power of Two Choices work stealing

Replace sequential linear scan in steal_task() with the "Power of Two
Choices" algorithm (Mitzenmacher 2001). Instead of starting at a random
index and probing every queue, we:

1. Select two distinct random candidates
2. Query their lengths to find the more loaded queue
3. Attempt steal from the heavier queue first
4. Fall back to the lighter queue if primary fails
5. Final linear scan fallback for edge cases

This reduces expected tail-latency from O(N) to O(1) for the common
case and minimizes lock contention since we only query two queues
instead of potentially all N.

local_queue.rs: Add Stealer::len() and Stealer::is_empty() methods
that take a short-lived lock to support the two-choices sampling.
Suitable for this use case since steal sampling occurs outside the
hot execution path.

three_lane.rs: Remove unused CURRENT_LOCAL thread-local and
ScopedLocalScheduler struct (dead code — no references remain).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
